nodes.name: KYC Intake Webhook
nodes.notes: Entry point. POST multipart/form-data with a 'profile' text field (stringified JSON) and an 'id_document' binary file. Responds immediately (Respond Mode: Immediately) so the client isn't blocked waiting for OCR + DB + Slack to finish.

nodes.name: Parse & Validate Submission
nodes.notes: Minimizes PII exposure downstream by extracting only applicant_id/email/phone instead of passing the whole raw form body forward. Throws on malformed/missing data so bad submissions never reach the OCR API.

nodes.name: Normalize OCR Data
nodes.notes: Flattens Mindee's nested document.inference.prediction object into a clean, flat record. given_names/surnames are arrays in the Mindee schema (a person can have multiple), so we take index [0]. This is the only place in the workflow that should ever reference the raw Mindee response shape — everything downstream works off these clean field names.

nodes.name: KYC Validation Logic
nodes.notes: Age check is calendar-accurate (month/day aware), not a naive year subtraction. Also rejects records with an unreadable date or a missing document number instead of silently passing them.

nodes.name: Log KYC Attempt (Audit Trail)
nodes.notes: Writes every KYC attempt (pass or fail) to kyc_audit_log for compliance traceability, independent of whether the customer record itself gets inserted. onError is set to continueRegularOutput: a logging hiccup should never block a real applicant from being processed, but it will still show up as a failed sub-step in the n8n execution log.

nodes.name: Route: KYC Passed?
nodes.notes: True branch -> Verified customer insert. False branch -> manual review alert to #internal-control.

nodes.name: Insert Verified Customer
nodes.notes: Only first_name, last_name, date_of_birth and document_number are persisted to the long-lived customers table — no raw OCR payload, no document image, no submitted_email/phone unless your schema explicitly needs them. Errors route to the error output instead of failing the whole execution silently.

nodes.name: Alert #internal-control: Manual KYC Review Required
nodes.notes: Business-rule failure (underage / expired ID / unreadable doc) — routed to #internal-control for a human compliance reviewer. Distinct from the technical-failure alerts below, which indicate an API/DB problem rather than a flagged applicant.

nodes.name: Format OCR Failure Payload
nodes.notes: Runs only off the Mindee node's error output. Normalizes whatever error shape the HTTP node produced into a clean record for alerting.

nodes.name: Alert: OCR API Failure
nodes.notes: Technical alert distinct from the compliance/manual-review alert — lets engineering distinguish 'OCR API is down' from 'this specific applicant looks underage'.

nodes.name: Alert: Customer DB Insert Failed
nodes.notes: Critical: this fires when an applicant PASSED KYC but the DB write itself failed. Treated as higher urgency than the routine manual-review alert.

nodes.name: Mindee OCR: International ID Extraction
nodes.notes: Calls Mindee's pre-built International ID v2 model (covers national IDs, driver's licenses and passports). Document is sent as multipart binary form data; the binary itself never gets logged or stored anywhere else in this workflow. Authentication is handled entirely through n8n's stored credential (mindeeInvoiceApi) — no API key is ever hardcoded in this workflow file. On a hard failure (auth, rate limit, malformed image) the error output (index 1) routes to the failure-handling branch instead of stopping the workflow.
